ogTitle: Popanyinning vs Port Albany: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Popanyinning and Port Albany.
heading: Popanyinning vs Port Albany — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Popanyinning, Western Australia and Port Albany, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Popanyinning has a lower overall climate risk score (42/100) compared to Port Albany (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Popanyinning or Port Albany safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Popanyinning has a flood risk score of 51/100, while Port Albany scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Popanyinning scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Port Albany scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Popanyinning or Port Albany?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Popanyinning has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
